question: Engine 47 is showing elevated Ps30 readings — is this a known fault pattern, and what does the manual say to do?

before.answer: Engine 47 is showing elevated Ps30 readings, which is consistent with the FC-HPC-001 fault pattern. The manual indicates that HPC degradation is the dominant fault mode in this fleet, and the 'pressure-loss-first' presentation is a known signature of HPC degradation. The work order for Engine 47 notes a similar pattern with P30 falling below the threshold, which aligns with the FC-HPC-001 signature. The manual suggests evaluating the HPC signature first when in doubt.

after.answer: Engine 47 is showing a known fault pattern of HPC degradation. The elevated Ps30 reading is part of the primary signature for fault code FC-HPC-001, which indicates HPC efficiency loss. The manual advises scheduling a wash and clearance check when Ps30 is ≥ 48.10 psia and P30 is ≤ 551.16 psia, which matches the current conditions of Engine 47.

fix_note: Baseline retrieved an engine-named work order and a sensor *trend*, so it missed the manual/fault-code and under-reported the alarm. Two fixes — type-aware fusion retrieval and status-preferring routing — make it cite the canonical procedure and read the live alarm correctly.
